items.title: LIVE: Weer veranderingen verwacht bij Anderlecht, Union moet puzzelen in de aanval
items.summary: Vanavond om 18u30 staat er met RSC Anderlecht tegen Union Saint-Gilloise opnieuw een beladen Brusselse derby op het programma. Beide ploegen hebben iets recht te zetten na hun recente puntenverlies en trekken met duidelijke ambities het veld op in het Lotto Park.

items.title: WK-auditie Godts? 'Zlatan-vibes tijdens NAC-Ajax'
items.summary: In de nieuwe aflevering van VI ZSM blikken Bart Kruijt en Jarno Verweij terug op de wedstrijden van zaterdagavond in de Eredivisie, met speciale aandacht voor Ajax en Mika Godts.

items.title: "Tout le monde me dit de tirer plus..." : le déclic de Jérémy Doku arrive au bon moment
items.summary: Mené et bousculé par Southampton, Manchester City a arraché sa place en finale grâce à un Jérémy Doku étincelant. Entré en jeu, le Belge a renversé le match à lui seul.

items.title: Geen transfer voor sterspeler: Bayern van Vincent Kompany is bijzonder duidelijk
items.summary: Het Bayern van Vincent Kompany is kampioen en bevestigt nog maar eens zijn klasse. Zaterdag boog het zelfs een 3-0-achterstand tegen Mainz nog om.

items.title: Guardiola verklaart onderonsje met Reijnders: 'Ik zei dat ik hem wilde vermoorden'
items.summary: Pep Guardiola heeft tekst en uitleg gegeven bij een opmerkelijk moment met Tijjani Reijnders. Na de winnende 2-1 van Manchester City in de FA Cup-wedstrijd tegen Southampton rende de Spanjaard naar de net gewisselde Reijnders om hem in zijn armen te vliegen.

items.title: VI Live: uitgedund Eredivisie-programma, Chelsea in halve finale en kraker Italië
items.summary: Zondag staat er met twee duels een uitgedund Eredivisie-programma klaar. Excelsior neemt het op tegen FC Utrecht en FC Volendam gaat op bezoek bij Heracles Almelo. Gelukkig wordt er ook in het buitenland gevoetbald. Blijf op de hoogte met deze VI Live.

items.title: Franse media lyrisch over ingeving Luis Enrique: 'Reïncarnatie Pirlo en Busquets'
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain had zaterdag geen problemen met Angers . Zelfs met een B-ploeg werd er eenvoudig gewonnen bij de middenmoter en kan er zo fris toegewerkt worden naar de halve finale van de Champions League. Toch kreeg Luis Enrique na afloop veel complimenten, nadat een nieuwe ingeving van de Spanjaard voor een opvallende uitblinker zorgde.

items.title: Chelsea and Leeds head to Wembley, a big derby for Hearts and WCL action – matchday live
items.summary: ⚽ News, discussion and buildup before Sunday’s action ⚽ Leeds seek Wembley moment | Fixtures | Mail us here Arsenal, the holders, take on Lyon in the Women’s Champions League. Suzanne Wrack has spoken to Olivia Smith: “As the season reaches its climax, a Champions League semi-final against Lyon on Sunday is testament to how far up the ladder Smith has climbed. Now, she is heading towards unknown territory: a second season at the same club for the first time in her senior career. “I do feel quite calm now, knowing that I have set down some roots here, but at the end of the day, football is football and you never know what’s next,” she says. “So I’m always on my toes but, right now, I’m kind of laid-back, just enjoying the time here in the present with Arsenal and looking forward to winning more silverware and growing as a player and a person.” I sorted out my mind: “Come on, Gigi,” I said to myself, and I gave myself strength: “When the game is over you can stop playing football. Just take on this hour and a half and then say goodbye to it all.” Continue reading...

items.title: 'Club Brugge denkt aan Italiaanse verdediger met waarde van 10 miljoen euro'
items.summary: Club Brugge kijkt achter de schermen al vooruit naar volgend seizoen en speurt volop naar versterking. Volgens transferexpert Sacha Tavolieri is blauw-zwart daarbij uitgekomen bij een Italiaanse rechtsachter.

items.title: Traître pour les supporters, mais pas pour ses anciens joueurs ? Ivan Leko divise à La Gantoise
items.summary: Le retour d'Ivan Leko à Gand s'annonce bouillant ce dimanche. Si les supporters n'ont pas pardonné son départ à Bruges, ses anciens joueurs n'oublient pas l'impact qu'il a eu sur leur carrière.

items.title: Kuijt eerlijk over Feyenoord-huurling Carrillo: 'Ik had meer verwacht'
items.summary: Dirk Kuijt vindt dat Feyenoord-huurling Stéphano Carrillo tegenvalt in het shirt van FC Dordrecht. De Mexicaanse spits komt niet uit de verf in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie. Hoewel Kuijt zijn potentie erkent, geeft hij Carrillo wel een onvoldoende voor dit seizoen.

items.title: Doelpuntenmaker Aaron Bibout is duidelijk na nieuw puntenverlies van Genk
items.summary: Aaron Bibout, die het openingsdoelpunt maakte voor KRC Genk tegen Standard (1-1), sprak na de wedstrijd met ons. Bij de doelpuntenmaker overheerste vooral de frustratie, want hij dacht zijn ploeg op weg te hebben gezet naar de overwinning.

items.title: Le Bayern Munich et Vincent Kompany ont pris leur décision
items.summary: Le Bayern de Vincent Kompany est champion et confirme sa force. Mené 3-0 ce samedi, il a renversé Mayence.

items.title: Frank Boeckx ziet duidelijk probleem bij Gent voor Slag om Vlaanderen
items.summary: KAA Gent trekt met vertrouwen de Slag om Vlaanderen in na het punt op Union, maar tegen Club Brugge zal het volgens Frank Boeckx vooral offensief meer moeten brengen om echt kans te maken op de zege.

items.title: Carl Hoefkens transporté à l'hôpital après la défaite du NAC Breda face à l'Ajax
items.summary: Carl Hoefkens n'a pas pu se présenter devant la presse après la défaite contre l'Ajax Amsterdam et a été conduit à l'hôpital.

items.title: Un nouveau scandale secoue la Serie A : le patron des arbitres visé par une enquête
items.summary: Gianluca Rocchi est visé par une enquête pour fraude sportive et s'est autosuspendu après des soupçons liés à l'arbitrage en Italie.

items.title: ‘I felt fear I did not understand’: Buffon on the panic attack that threatened his career
items.summary: In this exclusive book extract, the former Italy goalkeeper describes a moment of crisis before a game against Reggina If I have to identify the most important moment of this crisis, it was just before a Juventus-Reggina match in February 2004. It was an evening game. We were six points off the top of the table. There were 13 games left in the season, so anything could still happen, but there was an air of negativity, as if the season was already over. We had just had two crazy and very different games. In our previous league match, we had conceded four goals to Totti and Cassano’s Roma, while in midweek we had won the Coppa Italia semi-final against Inter at San Siro, on penalties. Although we were still in the running in the Champions League and perhaps even a little in the league, inside me I was certain that in that season everything was lost. It was a classic winter Turin evening, wet and cold, and the stadium was half-empty. The speakers played a song that I only heard as an annoying buzz. During the warm-up I prayed and performed my usual pre-match routine, but it felt as if something was wrong with my muscles. After two minutes I put on my gloves, I stood in the goal and I realised that I was struggling to breathe. I stood there, staring at the pitch, and I felt slightly dizzy. What scared me, however, was the tightness I felt in my diaphragm, between chest and stomach, as if I had been hit. Continue reading...

items.title: Why being in an FA Cup semi-final means so much to Leeds fans
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at why the FA Cup semi-final against Chelsea means so much to long-suffering Leeds United fans.

items.title: 'It's not done' - Arsenal back up Rice's words with crucial win
items.summary: Arsenal deliver on Declan Rice's promise that the Premier League title race is "not done" with an important win over Newcastle.

items.title: Mikel Arteta criticises red card escapes for Newcastle’s Pope and City’s Khusanov
items.summary: ‘It’s a red card today. It’s a red card in Manchester’ Arsenal return to top of table by beating Newcastle Mikel Arteta has claimed that everything would be different for his Arsenal team in the Premier League title race if Manchester City had been reduced to 10 men in last Sunday’s pivotal game at the Eithad Stadium. The Arsenal manager insisted that the City defender, Abdukodir Khusanov, should have been sent off for a last-man foul on Kai Havertz in the 53rd minute with the score at 1-1. City went on to win 2-1 and Arteta said nothing after the game about an incident that did not generate much controversy, the consensus being Khusanov had defended his position fairly. Continue reading...

items.title: Trophyless to treble? Man City's quest for more history
items.summary: Manchester City were in a state of turmoil this time last year – staring at a first trophyless season since 2017 – but they are now in with a chance of a domestic treble.

items.title: Pep Guardiola says title race means Manchester City must prepare for ‘six cup finals’
items.summary: 2-1 win over Southampton secures fourth straight FA Cup final ‘Six games, we’ll be there, if we lose it’s over,’ says City manager Pep Guardiola praised his players and targeted “six cup finals” after Nico González’s 87th-minute winner knocked out Southampton and sent Manchester City to an unprecedented fourth successive FA Cup final. Tonda Eckert’s team were heading for a shock victory via Finn Azaz’s 79th-minute strike. But just three minutes later the substitute Jérémy Doku equalised before González’s long-range winner. Continue reading...

items.title: Three goals, eight chaotic minutes - how day of relegation drama unfolded
items.summary: On a day of twists and turns, Tottenham and West Ham both picked up crucial wins in their fight to avoid relegation.

items.title: De Zerbi demands mentality shift at injury-hit Spurs: ‘We need to stay strong in the head’
items.summary: Solanke and Simons were taken off injured at Wolves Tottenham still in relegation zone because of West Ham win Robert De Zerbi is waiting on the fitness of two more players, after Dominic Solanke and Xavi Simons were forced off injured at Molineux on Saturday, but he is optimistic that Tottenham’s 1-0 victory at Wolves “can change something in our mentality”. The Spurs head coach celebrated his first win in charge of the relegation-threatened Europa League holders with the visiting fans in the Steve Bull Stand, exchanging fist pumps and belief. Continue reading...

items.title: Has Salah played last Liverpool game? Slot says 'wait and see'
items.summary: Liverpool will have to "wait and see" whether Mohamed Salah has played his final game for the club, says manager Arne Slot.

items.title: How long can India ignore Sooryavanshi - and could he be unleashed on England?
items.summary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi wrote his name into the history books by becoming the youngest IPL centurion - just nine days after his record‑breaking debut as a 14‑year‑old in 2025.

items.title: Record London Marathon ahead: Route, start times and is Harry Styles running?
Fitzpatrick brothers take four-shot lead in Zurich Classic
items.summary: Brothers Alex and Matt Fitzpatrick opened a four-stroke lead heading into the final round of the Zurich Classic of New Orleans after shooting a tournament-record better-ball third round of 57.

items.title: Korda remains five shots clear at Chevron Championship ahead of final round
items.summary: Nelly Korda remains five shots clear at the Chevron Championship and in poll position ahead of the final round despite failing to reach the heights of her stunning opening two rounds.

items.title: Duckett makes first half-century of season for Nottinghamshire
items.summary: Ben Duckett made his first half-century of the season for Nottinghamshire to help strengthen his position at the top of England's Test order.
